headline.label: Taker USD volume
headline.caveat: Taker side only — maker flow and identities unobserved.

headline.label: Smart cohort ROI
headline.caveat: In-sample selection — the cohort is picked BY performance.

headline.label: Dumb cohort ROI
headline.caveat: Same in-sample caveat; small samples, fade with care.

headline.label: Overall buy pressure
headline.caveat: Every taker BUY has an unseen maker on the other side.

headline.label: Price jumps detected
headline.caveat: Move-based detection, unattributed to specific match events.

headline.label: Largest wallet
headline.caveat: Gross = both sides of taker fills; not net exposure.

cohorts.definition: Qualifying wallets: >=5 trades AND >=$200 gross USD (n=30731). Score = z(ROI) + z(informedness_cents), z-scored within the qualifying set (wallets with null informedness contribute 0 to that term). Smart = top max(10, n/10) wallets by score, dumb = bottom, both capped at n/2 so the cohorts stay disjoint (k=3073 here). ROI includes mark-to-market on open positions at last trade price. In-sample, WC-only.

archetypes.label: Scalper / MM-like
archetypes.description: >=100 trades, balanced buy/sell flow (35-65% buy USD), median trade <$100

notes: Jump = print >=6c from the median of the previous 5 prints on the market's primary token, extending while prints progress >=1c in the jump direction within 120s of the last progressing print; reprice_s = last progressing print minus first (0 = single-print jump, still counted). After each jump the reference window is re-seeded with post-jump prints and any re-trigger on the same market within 300s is merged into the same episode, so n_jumps counts distinct repricing episodes, not echoes of one move.
Only markets with >=30 captured fills are scanned; thin markets and truncated histories (324 markets hit the API history cap — see window.truncated_markets) under-count jumps.

Cross-market rows: each futures follower episode appears at most once, matched to the nearest preceding match-1X2 jump on the same team within 30 minutes (newest 20 shown); Draw markets are skipped (no team to propagate to).

honesty_notes: Taker-side data only: data-api /trades names the taker wallet on each fill; maker identities (resting orders, market makers) are never seen, so buy_pressure and all wallet stats describe aggressive flow only.

The trades API hard-caps history at 3,000 rows offset per market; 324 markets hit that cap (listed in window.truncated_markets) and only carry a deep-history sweep of LARGE trades beyond it — so small-trade stats there are incomplete AND per-wallet PnL/ROI/win-rate can be missing legs entirely for wallets active in those markets. Leaderboard rows carry partial_history=true when affected; 2 wallets have had their complete history re-swept via the per-user endpoint and are exempt.
Open-position marks are LAST-TRADE prints, not order-book mids — thin tokens can carry stale or manipulated marks into mtm_pnl and ROI.
Jump detection is purely price-move based and is NOT attributed to specific match events (no goal-timestamp feed integrated); some 'jumps' are liquidity gaps rather than news.
Smart/dumb cohorts are in-sample, WC-only performance ranks on small per-wallet samples with survivorship effects — treat as descriptive, not as a copy-trade signal.
